description: I heard a weeBEo raspy song x 2. I found the bird about 30 feet away. It was a "Traill's Flycatcher". The back, crown, and auriculars were green contrasting with a white throat. The primary extension was long. The wingbars and edging to tertials/secondaries were bright, contrasting sharply with blackish base-color to folded wings. There was a hint of a round eyering present. The bird sang weeBEo (accent on second syllable, third syllable soft) one more and then the bird flew off. I relocated it briefly a couple more times, but no additional marks were seen
